title: Verdict
description: The aggregated outcome of running a hypothesis through the committed gauntlet (net_of_cost -> baselines -> deflated_sharpe -> block_bootstrap -> cpcv_pbo -> haircut -> surrogate -> holdout). The verdict label is decided by which gate (if any) binds. SURVIVE = every gate passes. PROMISING = passes the core economic gates (net_of_cost, baselines, surrogate, holdout) but trips a multiple-testing / Deflated-Sharpe gate (the structure is real; the honest-N magnitude significance is not). KILL = fails a core economic gate (most hypotheses end here, and that is the point). DEFERRED = not run because the only honest test needs data unavailable at $0. INDETERMINATE = ran but the evidence is too weak/seed-sensitive to assign a confident label.

hypothesisId.description: Stable identifier for the hypothesis / strategy under test.

bindingGate.description: The id of the FIRST gate that failed (the binding constraint), or null when every gate passed (SURVIVE) or when the verdict is DEFERRED (never run).

trialCount.description: The HONEST N — the true number of distinct configs searched, used to deflate. Never below 1.

gates.description: Every gate's outcome, in the binding evaluation order.

rationale.description: One-line human explanation of why this label was assigned.

createdAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the verdict was produced.
